issue: Five accessory rules (haus.radix-shadcn-patterns, haus.tailwind-scss-patterns, haus.tanstack-query-router-patterns, haus.storybook-patterns, haus.playwright-patterns) are selected at medium confidence purely by repo-role-match against next-app/react-app even though the package.json has none of the corresponding dependencies (radix, shadcn, tanstack, storybook, tailwind absent; playwright is present so OK).
expected: Accessory rules should only fire when an actual dep/tag signal also matches. A bare next-app should select nextjs-patterns + react19-patterns + typescript6-patterns + (any actually-detected accessory rules).
rootCauseHypothesis: Many accessory catalog entries set `repoRoles: ["next-app", "react-app"]` so repo-role-match (+40) alone clears the 40-point cutoff. There is no rule that requires at least one dep/stack signal alongside the role.
